title: arctostaphylos crustacea subsp. eastwoodiana
intro: Arctostaphylos crustacea subsp. eastwoodiana, also known as Eastwood's manzanita, is a rare and endemic shrub native to California, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, leathery leaves. Traditionally, Eastwood's manzanita has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of polyphenolic compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Eastwood's manzanita can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
